species.citation: Willd. , Hort. Berol. (Willdenow) 1 (1806) t.7; Type: from Egypt. = Ammannia multiflora Roxb. , Fl. Ind. (Carey & Wallich) 1 (1820) 447 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 136; Type: from India. = Ammannia auriculata var. arenaria (Kunth) Koehne , Bot. Jahrb. Syst. 1 (1880) 245; ≡ Ammannia arenaria Kunth , Nov. Gen. Sp. (H.B.K.), quarto Ed. 6 (1824) 190. = Ammannia auriculata forma hyrcanica Koehne , Bot. Jahrb. Syst. 1 (1880) 245 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 135.
species.distribution: Afghanistan, Africa, America, Australia, Cambodia, Caribbean, China, Middle East, Nepal, Pakistan, Philippines, Solomon Isls, Thailand, Vietnam. LUZON: Abra, Bulacan, La Union, Laguna, Pampanga.
species.notes: Low elevation. Known from La Union from Lete 497.

species.citation: L. , Sp. Pl., 2nd Ed. (1762) 175 ; Merr., Fl. Manila (1912) 343; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 135; Type: from China. = Ammannia debilis Blanco , Fl. Filip., 2nd Ed. (1845) 192, nom. illeg., non Aiton. = Celosia nana Blanco , Fl. Filip., 1st Ed. (1837) 192. = Ammannia baccifera var. viridis (Willd. ex Hornem.) Koehne , Bot. Jahrb. Syst. 1 (1880) 260 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 136; ≡ Ammannia viridis Willd. ex Hornem. , Hort. Bot. Hafn. (1813) 146.
species.distribution: Africa, Australia, S & SE Asia. LUZON: Abra, Bulacan, Cagayan (photos), Ilocos Norte, NCR, Nueva Ecija, Pampanga, Pangasinan, Quezon, Rizal, MINDANAO: Davao Oriental, PANAY (photos).
species.notes: Throughout the Philippines. Low and medium elevation in open damp wet places. Often abundant.

species.citation: Rottb. , Pl. Horti Univ. Rar. Progr. (1773) 7 ; De Wilde & Duyfjes, Fl. Males. 1, 22 (2016) 12; Type: from Jamaica (neo).
species.distribution: America. LUZON.
India, Java, Sri Lanka.
species.notes: Reported for the Philippines by Moody (1989) as a weed in rice fields, but no specimens cited and not mentioned for the Philippines by De Wilde & Duyfjes (2016).

species.citation: (Jacq.) J.F.Macbr. , Publ. Field Mus. Nat. Hist., Bot. Ser. 8 (1930) 124; ≡ Lythrum carthagenense Jacq. , Enum. Syst. Pl. (1760) 22; Type: from Barbados.
species.distribution: America. MINDANAO: Bukidnon (photos), Misamis Oriental (photos), PANAY (photos).

species.citation: Blume , Mus. Bot. 2 (1856) 109 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 139; Backer & Steen., Fl. Males. ser. 1, 4 (1951) 288.
species.distribution: Borneo, Java, Lesser Sunda Isls, Moluccas, New Guinea, Philippines, Sulawesi. BALABAC, CAMIGUIN (photos), CATANDUANES, LEYTE, LUZON: Abra, Apayao, Aurora, Bulacan, Camarines Sur, Cavite, Ilocos Norte, Ilocos Sur, Isabela, Laguna, Quezon, Rizal, Sorsogon, MASBATE, MINDANAO: Agusan del Norte, Bukidnon (photos), Cotabato, Davao, Davao Oriental, Davao del Sur, Misamis Occidental, South Cotabato, Sultan Kudarat (photos), Zamboanga, Zamboanga del Sur, MINDORO, NEGROS (photos), PALAWAN, PANAY (photos).

genera.last_edited: Last edited by Pieter B. Pelser, 1 May 2025

species.citation: Blume , Mus. Bot. 2 (1856) 127 ; De Wilde & Duyfjes, Fl. Males. 1, 22 (2016) 29; Type: from Sulawesi (lecto). = Lagerstroemia piriformis Koehne , Bot. Jahrb. Syst. 4 (1883) 23 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 137; Furtado & Srisuko, Gard. Bull. Sing. 24 (1969) 248; Type: Cuming 1675 (B, holo, lost; BM, K, L, iso), Philippines. = Lagerstroemia batitinan S.Vidal , Revis. Pl. Vasc. Filip. (1886) 139; ≡ Lagerstroemia piriformis forma batitinan (S.Vidal) Furtado & Srisuko , Gard. Bull. Singapore 24 (1969) 254; Type: Vidal 356 bis (A, lecto; K, L, MA, isolecto), Luzon: Laguna, near San Antonio. = Lagerstroemia inopinata Furtado & Srisuko , Gard. Bull. Singapore 24 (1969) 238; Type: Ahern’s collector (Decades of Philip. Forest Flora no. 204) (A, holo; US, iso), Luzon: Rizal, Bosoboso. = Lagerstroemia alatulata Furtado & Srisuko , Gard. Bull. Singapore 24 (1969) 242; Type: PNH 8173 Sulit (A, holo; PNH, iso), Luzon: Laguna, Mt Makiling.
species.distribution: Borneo, Lesser Sunda Isls, Moluccas, New Guinea, Philippines, Sulawesi, Sumatra. LEYTE, LUZON: Albay, Cagayan, Isabela (photos), Laguna, Quezon, Rizal, MINDANAO: Agusan, Cotabato, Davao Oriental, Misamis, Surigao, Zamboanga del Norte, MINDORO, PANAY, PARASAN, SAMAR.

species.citation: (Turcz.) S.Vidal , Phan. Cuming. Philipp. (1885) 115 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 137; Furtado & Srisuko, Gard. Bull. Sing. 24 (1969) 228; ≡ Pterocalymma paniculata Turcz. , Bull. Soc. Imp. Naturalistes Moscou 19 (1846) 508; Type: Cuming 1188 (A, holo, ex LE; E, L, NY, SING, iso), Philippines. ≠ Lagerstroemia piriformis auct. non Koehne ; Merr., EPFP 3 (1923) 137, pro parte.
species.distribution: Endemic to the Philippines. BILIRAN, CATANDUANES, LUZON: Aurora, Cagayan, Camarines Sur, Ilocos Norte, Isabela, Laguna, Quezon (photos), Rizal, MINDANAO: Agusan del Norte (photos), PANAY (photos), SAMAR.

species.citation: Furtado & Srisuko , Gard. Bull. Singapore 24 (1969) 230; Type: BS 21959 Adduru (A, holo; L, UC, US, iso), Luzon: Abra.
species.distribution: Endemic to the Philippines. LUZON: Abra, Bulacan, Ilocos Sur.

species.citation: (L.) Pers. , Syn. Pl. (Pers.) 2 (1807) 72 ; Merr., Fl. Manila (1912) 341; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 137; Furtado & Srisuko, Gard. Bull. Sing. 24 (1969) 264; ≡ Munchausia speciosa L. , Hausvater 5 (1770) 357; Type: from China (lecto).
species.distribution: Borneo, Cambodia, China, India, Java, Laos, Lesser Sunda Isls, Malay Peninsula, Moluccas, Myanmar, Philippines, Sulawesi, Sumatra, Thailand, Vietnam. APULIT, BUSUANGA, CATANDUANES, CULION, GUIMARAS, LEYTE, LUZON: Abra (photos), Aurora, Bataan, Batangas, Bulacan, Camarines Sur, Cavite, Ilocos Norte, Isabela, La Union, Laguna, Mountain Province, NCR, Quezon, Rizal, Sorsogon, Tarlac, Zambales, MARINDUQUE, MINDANAO: Agusan, Agusan del Norte, Misamis Oriental (photos), Surigao, Zamboanga del Norte, MINDORO, PALAWAN, PANAY, POLILLO, SAMAR, TABLAS.
species.notes: Mostly in secondary forests at low and medium elevation. 0-500m to 700m (photos).

species.citation: Jack , Malayan Misc. 1 (1820) 38; Type: from Peninsular Malaysia (neo). var. floribunda. = Lagerstroemia floribunda var. brevifolia Craib, Fl. Siam. 1 (1931) 722; Furtado & Srisuko, Gard. Bull. Sing. 24 (1969) 331. Distribution: Cambodia, Laos, Malay Peninsula, Myanmar, Thailand, Vietnam. Notes: Not naturalized. Cultivated, not naturalized.
L. , Syst. Nat., 10th Ed. 2 (1759) 1076 ; Merr., Fl. Manila (1912) 341; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 136; Furtado & Srisuko, Gard. Bull. Sing. 24 (1969) 190; Type: from China (lecto).
species.distribution: China, Himalayas, Indochina, Japan.
species.notes: Widely cultivated all over the world. Not naturalized.

species.citation: Teijsm. & Binn. , Natuurk. Tijdschr. Ned.-Indië 25 (1863) 425 ; Furtado & Srisuko, Gard. Bull. Sing. 24 (1969) 303; Type: from Thailand.
species.distribution: Thailand. LUZON: Laguna.

species.citation: Koehne , Bot. Jahrb. Syst. 4 (1883) 20 ; Furtado & Srisuko, Gard. Bull. Sing. 24 (1969) 281; Type: from Taiwan (lecto).
species.distribution: China, Pacific Ocean, Taiwan. LUZON: Laguna.

species.citation: L. , Sp. Pl., 1st Ed. (1753) 349 ; Merr., Fl. Manila (1912) 342; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 138.
species.distribution: Africa, India, Middle East, Pakistan. PALAWAN.
species.notes: Introduced in most tropical countries. Cultivated as an ornamental in many parts of the Philippines. Scarcely naturalized. Henna.

species.citation: J.R.Forst. & G.Forst. , Char. Gen. Pl., 2nd Ed. (1776) 68 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 136; Romeroso et al., Asian J. Biol. Life Sc. 10 (2021) 438; Type: from French Polynesia (lecto).
species.distribution: Africa, Asia, Australia, Pacific Ocean, Philippines. APULIT, BASILAN, BOHOL, BURIAS, CEBU, CORON, DALUPIRI, DAMMAI, DANJUGAN, HOMONHON, HUNDRED ISLS (photos), JOLO, LUZON: Aurora, Batangas, Cagayan, Camarines Sur, Ilocos Norte, Isabela, Pangasinan (photos), Quezon, MALAMAWI, MARINDUQUE, MASBATE, MINDANAO: Davao Oriental (photos), Sultan Kudarat (photos), MINDORO, OLANGO, PALAWAN, PUJADA, SIARGAO, SIASI, SIQUIJOR (photos), TAWI-TAWI, TICAO, URSULA.

species.citation: L. , Sp. Pl., 1st Ed. (1753) 472 ; Merr., Fl. Manila (1912) 344; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 141; Backer, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 4 (1951) 226.
species.distribution: Middle East.

genera.last_edited: Last edited by Pieter B. Pelser, 23 September 2023

species.citation: Koehne , Bot. Jahrb. Syst. 1 (1880) 167 ; De Wilde & Duyfjes, Fl. Males. 1, 22 (2016) 57; Type: from Myanmar.
species.distribution: China, Java, Myanmar, Philippines. LUZON.

species.citation: (Willd.) Koehne , Bot. Jahrb. Syst. 1 (1880) 172 ; Merr., Fl. Manila (1912) 342; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 134; Leeuwen, Blumea 19 (1971) 54; ≡ Peplis indica Willd. , Sp. Pl., 4th Ed. 2 (1799) 244; Type: from India.
species.distribution: Asia, Java, Lesser Sunda Isls, Malay Peninsula, Philippines, Sulawesi, Sumatra. LUZON: Cavite, NCR, Rizal, Zambales, PALAUI (photos).

species.citation: Schltdl. & Cham. , Linnaea 5 (1830) 567 ; Merr., Fl. Manila (1912) 342; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 134; Leeuwen, Blumea 19 (1971) 54; Type: from Mexico. Common in warmer countries.
species.distribution: Java, New Guinea, Philippines. LUZON: Rizal, MINDANAO: Davao.

species.citation: (L.) Koehne , Fl. Bras. (Martius) 13(2) (1877) 194; ≡ Ammannia ramosior L. , Sp. Pl., 1st Ed. (1753) 120; Type: from N. America. = Ammannia catholica Cham. & Schltdl. , Linnaea 2 (1827) 378; ≡ Rotala catholica (Cham. & Schltdl.) Leeuwen , Blumea 19 (1971) 54 ; Merr., Fl. Manila (1912) 342; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 135; Type: Chamisso s.n. (LE, hololecto; G, isolecto), Philippines: Luzon. = Ammannia monoflora Blanco , Fl. Filip., 1st Ed. (1837) 64; Type: Merrill 426 (L, neo), Luzon: Manila.
species.distribution: Neotropics. LUZON: Cagayan (photos).
species.notes: Introduced in the Old World, only found as yet in Malesia in the Philippines. Rice paddy weed (Merrill 1923).

species.citation: (Poir.) C.D.K.Cook ex H.Hara , Enum. Fl. Pl. Nepal 2 (1979) 173; ≡ Ammannia rosea Poir. , Encycl. (Lamarck) Suppl. 1 (1810) 329. = Rotala leptopetala (Blume) Koehne , Bot. Jahrb. Syst. 1 (1880) 162 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 135; ≡ Ammannia leptopetala Blume , Mus. Bot. 2 (1856) 134; Type: from Java. ≠ Rotala pentandra auct. non (Roxb.) Blatt. & Hallb. ; Leeuwen, Blumea 19 (1971) 55, pro parte, pro basion. et syn. R. leptopetala.
species.distribution: Asia, Java, Malay Peninsula, New Guinea, Philippines, Sulawesi, Sumatra. LUZON: Ilocos Norte, NCR, Rizal, MINDANAO: Davao Oriental.

genera.last_edited: Last edited by Pieter B. Pelser, 21 May 2025

species.citation: Sm. , Cycl. 33 (1816) n.2 ; Backer & Steen., Fl. Males. ser. 1, 4 (1951) 285; Cortez et al., Check List 19 (2023) 988. ≠ Sonneratia caseolaris auct. non (L.) Engl. ; Merr., EPFP 3 (1923) 139, pro parte.
species.distribution: Africa, Andaman Isls, Australia, Bangladesh, Bismarck Arch, Borneo, Cambodia, China, Indian Ocean, Java, Lesser Sunda Isls, Malay Peninsula, Moluccas, Myanmar, New Caledonia, New Guinea, Nicobar Isls, Pacific Ocean, Philippines, Ryukyu Isls, Solomon Isls, Sri Lanka, Sulawesi, Sumatra, Thailand, Vietnam. ALABAT, BOAAN, BORACAY (photos), CEBU (photos), CORON, DANJUGAN (photos), HUNDRED ISLS (photos), LUZON: NCR, Quezon, MINDANAO: Cotabato, Davao Occidental (photos), Davao Oriental (photos), Lanao del Norte (photos), Misamis, MINDORO, NEGROS (photos), OLANGO, PALASAN (photos), PALAWAN, PANAY (photos), SEMIRARA, SIARGAO.
species.notes: Shallow parts of calm seas, seashores, along the mouth of tidal crees. Prefers salt water and grows well on sandy or rocky as on a muddy soil, not rarely on coral terraces. Often gregarious and predominating, but usually not forming a dense growth, except sometimes where better fuel-trees have been destroyed. 0m.

species.citation: (L.) Engl. , Nat. Pflanzenfam. Nachtr. 1 (1897) 261 ; Merr., Fl. Manila (1912) 344; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 139; Backer & Steen., Fl. Males. ser. 1, 4 (1951) 283; Cortez et al., Check List 19 (2023) 988; Sitoy & Buenavista, Reinwardtia 23 (2024) 5; ≡ Rhizophora caseolaris L. , Herb. Amboin. (Linn.) (1754) 13. = Sonneratia acida L.f. , Suppl. Pl. (1782) 252 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 139. = Sonneratia pagatpat Blanco , Fl. Filip., 1st Ed. (1837) 424 ; Merr., Fl. Manila (1912) 344; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 139.
species.distribution: Andaman Isls, Australia, Bangladesh, Bismarck Arch, Borneo, Cambodia, China, India, Java, Lesser Sunda Isls, Malay Peninsula, Moluccas, New Caledonia, New Guinea, Nicobar Isls, Pacific Ocean, Pakistan, Philippines, Solomon Isls, Sri Lanka, Sulawesi, Sumatra, Thailand, Vietnam. APULIT, CORON, MINDANAO: Misamis Oriental, NEGROS (photos), PALAWAN, SIARGAO.
species.notes: Less salt parts of mangrove forests on a deeply muddy soil, never on coral bank, often along tidal creeks with slow moving water and ascending these as far as the flood mounts. Sea level. 0m.

species.citation: N.C.Duke , Austrobaileya 2 (1984) 103.
species.distribution: Australia, Borneo, China, Pacific Ocean, Philippines, Solomon Isls. NEGROS (photos).
species.notes: Hybrid between Sonneratia alba and Sonneratia caseolaris.

species.citation: Backer , Bull. Jard. Bot. Buitenzorg, 3rd Sér. 2 (1920) 329 ; Backer & Steen., Fl. Males. ser. 1, 4 (1951) 285; Primavera et al. (2004) Handb. Mangrove Philip. Panay 76.
species.distribution: Andaman Isls, Borneo, Cambodia, China, Java, Malay Peninsula, Moluccas, New Guinea, Philippines, Solomon Isls, Sulawesi, Thailand, Vietnam. LEYTE, LUZON: Batangas (photos), POLILLO.
species.notes: Landward side of tidal forests in the less salt part on a muddy soil, along tidal creeks, never on coral reefs. Locally numerous, but on the whole, rather rare. Sea level.

genera.last_edited: Last edited by Pieter B. Pelser, 5 January 2020

species.citation: (L.) Kurz , J. Asiat. Soc. Bengal, Pt. 2, Nat. Hist. 40 (1871) 56 ; De Wilde & Duyfjes, Fl. Males. 1, 22 (2016) 64; ≡ Lythrum fruticosum L. , Syst. Nat., 10th Ed. 2 (1759) 1045; Type: from China (lecto).
species.distribution: Bhutan, China, India, Java, Laos, Lesser Sunda Isls, Madagascar, Madura, Myanmar, Nepal, Pakistan, Philippines, Sulawesi, Thailand.
